description: Doctoral-level cognitive psychology pack covering attention, memory, learning, reasoning, executive control, metacognition, and evidence-based explanation.

theories.name: Working Memory Model
theories.oneLiner: Working memory has limited capacity and is responsible for short-term information manipulation.

faceExamples.title: Overloaded founder
faceExamples.scenario: User juggling multiple technical layers at once.

keywords: Working memory has limited capacity and is responsible for short-term information manipulation

definitionSurface.definition: Working memory has limited capacity and is responsible for short-term information manipulation.

theories.name: Cognitive Load Theory
theories.oneLiner: Learning efficiency declines when intrinsic and extraneous load exceed processing capacity.

faceExamples.title: Documentation overload
faceExamples.scenario: User overwhelmed by too many moving system components.

keywords: Learning efficiency declines when intrinsic and extraneous load exceed processing capacity
intentSignals: why is this hard to learn

definitionSurface.definition: Learning efficiency declines when intrinsic and extraneous load exceed processing capacity.

theories.name: Dual Process Theory
theories.oneLiner: Human reasoning operates through fast intuitive (System 1) and slow analytical (System 2) processes.
actionablePrinciples: Run a slow-pass review on major moves.

faceExamples.title: Reactive decision
faceExamples.scenario: User wants to pivot immediately after frustration.

keywords: Human reasoning operates through fast intuitive (System 1) and slow analytical (System 2) processes

definitionSurface.definition: Human reasoning operates through fast intuitive (System 1) and slow analytical (System 2) processes.

theories.name: Metacognition
theories.oneLiner: Thinking about one's own thinking improves strategy and learning efficiency.

faceExamples.title: Overconfidence check
faceExamples.scenario: User believes they have mastered a domain quickly.

keywords: Thinking about one's own thinking improves strategy and learning efficiency

definitionSurface.definition: Thinking about one's own thinking improves strategy and learning efficiency.

theories.name: Spaced Repetition
theories.oneLiner: Information retained better when exposure is spaced over time.
actionablePrinciples: Use retrieval practice instead of rereading.

faceExamples.title: Skill retention
faceExamples.scenario: User forgets system structures after short bursts.

keywords: Information retained better when exposure is spaced over time

definitionSurface.definition: Information retained better when exposure is spaced over time.

theories.name: Attention Control Theory
theories.oneLiner: Anxiety and stress reduce attentional control and working memory efficiency.
actionablePrinciples: Stabilize emotional state before complex reasoning.

faceExamples.title: High-pressure launch
faceExamples.scenario: User under deadline cannot think clearly.

keywords: Anxiety and stress reduce attentional control and working memory efficiency

definitionSurface.definition: Anxiety and stress reduce attentional control and working memory efficiency.

candidates.neutralDefinition: Attention is selective focus: the process of prioritizing some information while filtering or down-weighting other information.
candidates.clarificationQuestion: Do you mean human attention in cognition, or attention mechanisms in AI models?

applicationRules.instruction: Use cognitive explanations as educational framing, not medical diagnosis.
When a theory is surfaced, include at least one actionable principle.
If cognitive load is high, simplify theory explanation.

meta.intendedUse: Cognitive reasoning support layer for Marion psychology domain.

trustRules: Prefer meta-analyses and systematic reviews over isolated studies for settled questions.
Prefer official competency/guideline sources for curricular and training claims.
Treat textbook summaries as synthesis, not as substitutes for primary evidence.
Separate measurement validity from popularity of an instrument.

nyx: Use advanced ideas in plain language without sounding clinical or detached.
Prefer conceptual compression: mechanism → implication → next question.
Avoid sounding diagnostic when discussing emotions, habits, or conflict.
marion: Weight claims by evidence tier and measurement quality.
Separate theory explanation, evidence appraisal, and action suggestions into distinct layers.
Use retrieval routing to pull safety pack before intervention content when risk language appears.

doctoralModules.title: Theory, history, and paradigm formation
outcomes: Explain how paradigms rise, fragment, and get revised.
Distinguish descriptive, mechanistic, and normative claims.

doctoralModules.title: Methods, measurement, and statistical reasoning
outcomes: Critique design validity, power, and measurement quality.
Interpret effect sizes and uncertainty correctly.
Identify confounds, bias pathways, and causal limits.

doctoralModules.title: Translation, ethics, and applied reasoning
outcomes: Translate evidence into bounded practical guidance.
Separate educational explanation from diagnosis or treatment.

writtenComps: Compare two major theoretical frameworks and defend which better explains a class of findings.
Critique a study program as if reviewing a dissertation proposal.
Integrate theory, method, and ethics in a high-stakes applied scenario.
oralDefensePrompts: What evidence would falsify your preferred explanation?
What measurement choices most threaten interpretation here?
How should an assistant explain this to a layperson without overstating certainty?
specialtyPrompts: Defend or reject the claim that working memory is best treated as a unitary bottleneck.
Explain when dual-process accounts illuminate judgment and when they obscure mechanism.
Design a study to test whether a learning intervention improves transfer rather than just short-term performance.

caseStudies.title: Operator overload in a multichannel system
questions: What is most likely failing first?
How would a lab paradigm operationalize this?

caseStudies.title: Strong confidence after shallow exposure
questions: What data would distinguish real mastery from familiarity?

caseStudies.title: Fast forgetting after intense short bursts
questions: What schedule or measurement plan best tests retention?

projects.title: Cognitive-load explainer engine
projects.deliverable: Rule set that translates complex tasks into chunked, low-load steps for Nyx.

projects.title: Memory-support toolkit
projects.deliverable: JSON modules for retrieval practice, spacing, cueing, and interference reduction.

assessmentFamilies.name: working_memory_tasks
assessmentFamilies.notes: Describe tasks conceptually; do not oversell any single task as complete measurement.

keywords: Describe tasks conceptually; do not oversell any single task as complete measurement

definitionSurface.definition: Describe tasks conceptually; do not oversell any single task as complete measurement.

assessmentFamilies.name: executive_control_tasks
assessmentFamilies.notes: Useful for operationalization examples and doctoral methods discussions.

keywords: Useful for operationalization examples and doctoral methods discussions

definitionSurface.definition: Useful for operationalization examples and doctoral methods discussions.

officialOrOpenResources: Open psychology textbooks for baseline definitions

runtimeMetadata.normalizedFor: Nyx/Marion psychology domain definition inventory sweep

entryConditions: route through clinical safety first for crisis, diagnosis, self-harm, mania/psychosis, eating-disorder, or overdose language
notes: Prefer bounded explanation over diagnostic certainty.
Escalate to safety-first handling when risk signals are present.
Use source index for trust weighting and bibliographic routing.

blockedFallbacks: I need one more concrete detail to route this cleanly.
I’ll keep this broad and practical instead of forcing the wrong specialist angle too early.
The specific technical move is to name the active component.

cognitive intelligence.neutralDefinition: Cognitive intelligence generally means the capacity to understand information, reason through problems, learn from patterns, adapt to context, and make useful decisions.
cognitive intelligence.clarificationQuestion: Do you mean human cognition in psychology, or AI/system intelligence in machine reasoning?

reasoning.neutralDefinition: Reasoning is the process of using information, evidence, and rules to reach a conclusion or choose an action.
reasoning.clarificationQuestion: Do you mean human cognitive reasoning, AI reasoning behavior, or legal reasoning?

bias.neutralDefinition: Bias is a systematic tilt in attention, judgment, measurement, or interpretation that can distort conclusions.
bias.clarificationQuestion: Do you mean cognitive bias in people, model/data bias in AI, or research/measurement bias?

validation.neutralDefinition: Validation means checking whether something is accurate, reliable, appropriate, or fit for its intended purpose.
validation.clarificationQuestion: Do you mean research-measurement validity, AI output validation, cybersecurity input validation, or emotional validation?

resilience.neutralDefinition: Resilience is the ability to recover, adapt, and keep functioning under stress, setback, or disruption.
resilience.clarificationQuestion: Do you mean psychological resilience, business resilience, or financial resilience?

identity.neutralDefinition: Identity is the pattern of self-understanding, roles, values, memories, and group memberships that helps a person answer who they are.
identity.clarificationQuestion: Do you mean personal identity, social identity, developmental identity, or Nyx/Marion system identity?

attention.neutralDefinition: Attention is selective focus: the process of prioritizing some information while filtering or down-weighting other information.
attention.clarificationQuestion: Do you mean human attention in cognition, or attention mechanisms in AI models?

learning.neutralDefinition: Learning is a change in knowledge, behavior, skill, or predictive capacity through experience, feedback, or practice.
learning.clarificationQuestion: Do you mean human learning, machine learning, or instructional learning design?

confidence.neutralDefinition: Confidence can mean subjective certainty, statistical precision, model certainty, or decision conviction depending on the domain.
confidence.clarificationQuestion: Do you mean personal confidence, statistical confidence, AI confidence, or financial confidence/risk?

compliance.neutralDefinition: Compliance means conforming to a request, rule, authority, standard, or required behavior.
compliance.clarificationQuestion: Do you mean social compliance in psychology, legal/regulatory compliance, cybersecurity compliance, or business process compliance?

items.definition: Working memory has limited capacity and is responsible for short-term information manipulation.
Learning efficiency declines when intrinsic and extraneous load exceed processing capacity.
Human reasoning operates through fast intuitive (System 1) and slow analytical (System 2) processes.
Thinking about one's own thinking improves strategy and learning efficiency.
Information retained better when exposure is spaced over time.
Anxiety and stress reduce attentional control and working memory efficiency.
Describe tasks conceptually; do not oversell any single task as complete measurement.
Useful for operationalization examples and doctoral methods discussions.
